title: Crunchy Herbal Spanish Ricotta Salad
description: A vibrant, FODMAP-friendly salad that pairs creamy ricotta with fresh herbs, crunchy toasted nuts, and tangy pickled cucumber for a quick weeknight lunch.

ingredients.name: mixed fresh herbs (parsley, cilantro, oregano, basil)
sea salt and cracked black pepper

instructions: Thinly slice the pickled cucumber and julienne the carrot for ribbons.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and slice the olives.
In a shallow bowl, whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to make a light vinaigrette.
Place the ricotta in a larger bowl and fold in the chopped herbs, then gently stir in about half of the vinaigrette.
Add the tomatoes, carrot ribbons, pickled cucumber, and olives to the bowl, tossing lightly to combine.
Toast the pine nuts in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden and fragrant, about 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently to avoid burning.
Fold the toasted pine nuts into the salad, reserving a small handful for garnish.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or lemon juice if needed.
Serve the salad chilled or at room temperature, garnished with the remaining toasted pine nuts.
Optional: add a sprinkle of crumbled feta or a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil for extra richness.
